title: betula pubescens subsp. odorata
intro: Betula pubescens subsp. odorata, also known as the sweet birch, is a subspecies of birch tree that is native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its distinctive sweet and resinous bark. The medicinal properties of the sweet birch include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and oral infections. The health benefits of consuming sweet birch tea or using its oil topically include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and soothing digestive issues, making it a popular natural remedy. Herbal preparations of sweet birch include tea, essential oil, and salves, which can be used internally or applied topically to reap its medicinal benefits.
